devanagari: श्रीमद्भागवत महापुराण । सप्तमः स्कंधःचतुर्थोऽध्यायः । हिरण्यकशिपोः शासनम् प्रह्रादस्य जन्म तद्गुणानां च वर्णनम्नारद उवाचएवं वृतः शतधृतिः ...
english_devnagari: śrīmadbhāgavata mahāpurāṇasaptamaḥ skaṃdhaḥcaturtho'dhyāyaḥhiraṇyakaśipoḥ śāsanam prahrādasya janma tadguṇānāṃ ca varṇanamnārada uvācaevaṃ vṛtaḥ śatadhṛtiḥ hiraṇyakaśiporathaprādāt tat tapasā prīto varān tasya sudurlabhān
translation:
Nārada said:
Thus solicited, Brahmā who was extremely pleased with the austere penance of Hiraṇyakaśipu, granted on him the boons which are very rarely obtained by anyone.

devanagari: ब्रह्मोवाचतातेमे दुर्लभाः पुंसां यान् वृणीषे वरान् मम । तथापि वितराम्यङ्ग वरान् यदपि दुर्लभान् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmovācatāteme durlabhāḥ puṃsāṃ yān vṛṇīṣe varān mamatathāpi vitarāmyaṅga varān yadapi durlabhān
translation:
Brahmā said:
“My dear child! These boons that you seek of me are very rarely to be obtained by men. I, however, confer them on you, even though they are very difficult to be secured.”

devanagari: ततो जगाम भगवान् अमोघानुग्रहो विभुः । पूजितोऽसुरवर्येण स्तूयमानः प्रजेश्वरैः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: tato jagāma bhagavān amoghānugraho vibhuḥpūjito'suravaryeṇa stūyamānaḥ prajeśvaraiḥ
translation: Then the glorious almighty god of unfailing grace, who was worshipped by the king of Asuras, departed while being extolled by lords of creation (Prajāpatis like Marīci and others).

devanagari: एवं लब्धवरो दैत्यो बिभ्रद् हेममयं वपुः । भगवत्यकरोद् द्वेषं भ्रातुर्वधमनुस्मरन् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ labdhavaro daityo bibhrad hemamayaṃ vapuḥbhagavatyakarod dveṣaṃ bhrāturvadhamanusmaran
translation: The demon who was thus blessed with the boons, assumed a resplendent golden body. Constantly remembering the killing of his brother (by Viṣṇu), he cherished hatred towards the glorious Lord.

devanagari: स विजित्य दिशः सर्वा लोकांश्च त्रीन्महासुरः । देवासुरमनुष्येन्द्रान् गन्धर्वगरुडोरगान् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vijitya diśaḥ sarvā lokāṃśca trīnmahāsuraḥdevāsuramanuṣyendrān gandharvagaruḍoragān
translation: The great Asura subjugated and brought under his control all directions, the three worlds, gods, Asuras, the kings of men, Gandharvas, Garuḍa-like birds, serpents, demi-gods (like Siddhas, Cāraṇas, Vidyādharas); (he subdued) sages, Lords of manes (Pitṛs), Manus, Yakṣas, Rākṣasas, chiefs of Piśācas, Pretas and bhūtas (goblins, evil spirits etc.); having vanquished and brought under his sway all the chiefs of living beings, the conqueror of the universe forcibly usurped the regions (and offices) of the protectors of the world, along with their glory.

devanagari: सिद्धचारणविद्याध्रान् ऋषीन् पितृपतीन् मनून् । यक्षरक्षःपिशाचेशान् प्रेतभूतपतीनथ ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: siddhacāraṇavidyādhrān ṛṣīn pitṛpatīn manūnyakṣarakṣaḥpiśāceśān pretabhūtapatīnatha
translation: The great Asura subjugated and brought under his control all directions, the three worlds, gods, Asuras, the kings of men, Gandharvas, Garuḍa-like birds, serpents, demi-gods (like Siddhas, Cāraṇas, Vidyādharas); (he subdued) sages, Lords of manes (Pitṛs), Manus, Yakṣas, Rākṣasas, chiefs of Piśācas, Pretas and bhūtas (goblins, evil spirits etc.); having vanquished and brought under his sway all the chiefs of living beings, the conqueror of the universe forcibly usurped the regions (and offices) of the protectors of the world, along with their glory.

devanagari: सर्वसत्त्वपतीन्जित्वा वशमानीय विश्वजित् । जहार लोकपालानां स्थानानि सह तेजसा ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvasattvapatīnjitvā vaśamānīya viśvajitjahāra lokapālānāṃ sthānāni saha tejasā
translation: The great Asura subjugated and brought under his control all directions, the three worlds, gods, Asuras, the kings of men, Gandharvas, Garuḍa-like birds, serpents, demi-gods (like Siddhas, Cāraṇas, Vidyādharas); (he subdued) sages, Lords of manes (Pitṛs), Manus, Yakṣas, Rākṣasas, chiefs of Piśācas, Pretas and bhūtas (goblins, evil spirits etc.); having vanquished and brought under his sway all the chiefs of living beings, the conqueror of the universe forcibly usurped the regions (and offices) of the protectors of the world, along with their glory.

devanagari: यत्र विद्रुमसोपाना महामारकता भुवः । यत्र स्फाटिककुड्यानि वैदूर्यस्तम्भपङ्क्तयः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: yatra vidrumasopānā mahāmārakatā bhuvaḥyatra sphāṭikakuḍyāni vaidūryastambhapaṅktayaḥ
translation: He established himself in the celestial region which was beautified with the charming heavenly pleasure-garden (Nandana). He took up his residence in the palace of the great Indra, (which was) constructed by Viśvakarman, as the very centre of affluence, wealth and prosperity of the three worlds (OR the abode of the Lakṣmī of the three worlds). There, the flight of steps are built of coral, the pavement consists of precious emeralds, the walls of crystals and the rows of columns were of lapis lazuli. The canopies are of artistic workmanship; the seats were wrought with rubies; beddings white as the foam of milk, were fringed with wreaths of pearls; the heavenly damsels (of pearl-like teeth) filling the mansion here and there (everywhere), with the sweet jingling sound of their anklets (nūpuras), see the reflections of their faces on the floors of precious stones.

devanagari: यत्र चित्रवितानानि पद्मरागासनानि च । पयःफेननिभाः शय्या मुक्तादामपरिच्छदाः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: yatra citravitānāni padmarāgāsanāni capayaḥphenanibhāḥ śayyā muktādāmaparicchadāḥ
translation: He established himself in the celestial region which was beautified with the charming heavenly pleasure-garden (Nandana). He took up his residence in the palace of the great Indra, (which was) constructed by Viśvakarman, as the very centre of affluence, wealth and prosperity of the three worlds (OR the abode of the Lakṣmī of the three worlds). There, the flight of steps are built of coral, the pavement consists of precious emeralds, the walls of crystals and the rows of columns were of lapis lazuli. The canopies are of artistic workmanship; the seats were wrought with rubies; beddings white as the foam of milk, were fringed with wreaths of pearls; the heavenly damsels (of pearl-like teeth) filling the mansion here and there (everywhere), with the sweet jingling sound of their anklets (nūpuras), see the reflections of their faces on the floors of precious stones.

devanagari: कूजद्भिर्नूपुरैर्देव्यः शब्दयन्त्य इतस्ततः । रत्‍नस्थलीषु पश्यन्ति सुदतीः सुन्दरं मुखम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: kūjadbhirnūpurairdevyaḥ śabdayantya itastataḥrat‍nasthalīṣu paśyanti sudatīḥ sundaraṃ mukham
translation: He established himself in the celestial region which was beautified with the charming heavenly pleasure-garden (Nandana). He took up his residence in the palace of the great Indra, (which was) constructed by Viśvakarman, as the very centre of affluence, wealth and prosperity of the three worlds (OR the abode of the Lakṣmī of the three worlds). There, the flight of steps are built of coral, the pavement consists of precious emeralds, the walls of crystals and the rows of columns were of lapis lazuli. The canopies are of artistic workmanship; the seats were wrought with rubies; beddings white as the foam of milk, were fringed with wreaths of pearls; the heavenly damsels (of pearl-like teeth) filling the mansion here and there (everywhere), with the sweet jingling sound of their anklets (nūpuras), see the reflections of their faces on the floors of precious stones.

devanagari: तस्मिन्महेन्द्रभवने महाबलो । महामना निर्जितलोक एकराट् । रेमेऽभिवन्द्याङ्घ्रियुगः सुरादिभिः । प्रतापितैरूर्जितचण्डशासनः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tasminmahendrabhavane mahābalomahāmanā nirjitaloka ekarāṭreme'bhivandyāṅghriyugaḥ surādibhiḥpratāpitairūrjitacaṇḍaśāsanaḥ
translation: In that palace of Mahendra, the mighty, proud yet magnanimous Hiraṇyakaśipu, the vanquisher and the absolute ruler of the world, enjoyed himself. He ruled with a strong iron hand, making the oppressed gods and others worship his feet respectfully.

devanagari: तमङ्ग मत्तं मधुनोरुगन्धिना । विवृत्तताम्राक्षमशेषधिष्ण्यपाः । उपासतोपायनपाणिभिर्विना । त्रिभिस्तपोयोगबलौजसां पदम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: tamaṅga mattaṃ madhunorugandhināvivṛttatāmrākṣamaśeṣadhiṣṇyapāḥupāsatopāyanapāṇibhirvinātribhistapoyogabalaujasāṃ padam
translation: With the exception of the three gods (Brahmā, Viṣṇu and Śiva) all the Lokapālas (guardians of the world like Indra), with tributes and presents in their hands, waited upon him (Hiraṇyakaśipu) who was (always) intoxicated with strong-flavoured wines and had his reddish eyes rolling due to inebriation, yet who was the receptacle of penance, Yoga, power and energy, Oh King.

devanagari: जगुर्महेन्द्रासनमोजसा स्थितं । विश्वावसुस्तुम्बुरुरस्मदादयः । गन्धर्वसिद्धा ऋषयोऽस्तुवन्मुहुः । विद्याधरा अप्सरसश्च पाण्डव ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: jagurmahendrāsanamojasā sthitaṃviśvāvasustumbururasmadādayaḥgandharvasiddhā ṛṣayo'stuvanmuhuḥvidyādharā apsarasaśca pāṇḍava
translation: Viśvāvasu, Tumburu, I and others (had to) give musical performance in the presence of him who had forcibly established himself on the throne of Mahendra. Gandharvas, Siddhas, sages, Vidyādharas, and celestial nymphs eulogised him, Oh descendant of Pāṇḍu.

devanagari: स एव वर्णाश्रमिभिः क्रतुभिर्भूरिदक्षिणैः । इज्यमानो हविर्भागान् अग्रहीत् स्वेन तेजसा ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa eva varṇāśramibhiḥ kratubhirbhūridakṣiṇaiḥijyamāno havirbhāgān agrahīt svena tejasā
translation: By his majestic lustre, he alone appropriated the portions of oblations offered in sacrifices with generous sacrificial gifts, performed by persons of various Varṇas (classes of society) and āśramas (stages in life).

devanagari: अकृष्टपच्या तस्यासीत् सप्तद्वीपवती मही । तथा कामदुघा गावो नानाश्चर्यपदं नभः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: akṛṣṭapacyā tasyāsīt saptadvīpavatī mahītathā kāmadughā gāvo nānāścaryapadaṃ nabhaḥ
translation: The earth with all the seven continents yielded harnest without ploughing (and other agricultural operations). The heaven offered him whatever he desired, and the sky presented him various wonderful objects.

devanagari: रत्‍नाकराश्च रत्‍नौघान् तत्पत्‍न्यश्चोहुरूर्मिभिः । क्षारसीधुघृतक्षौद्र दधिक्षीरामृतोदकाः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: rat‍nākarāśca rat‍naughān tatpat‍nyaścohurūrmibhiḥkṣārasīdhughṛtakṣaudra dadhikṣīrāmṛtodakāḥ
translation: Oceans (lit. mines of precious stones) full of salt water, wine, clarified butter, sugarcane juice, curds, milk and nectar-like (sweet) water, along with their wives (rivers falling in them) bore, on the crest of their waves, heaps of jewels.

devanagari: शैला द्रोणीभिराक्रीडं सर्वर्तुषु गुणान् द्रुमाः । दधार लोकपालानां एक एव पृथग्गुणान् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: śailā droṇībhirākrīḍaṃ sarvartuṣu guṇān drumāḥdadhāra lokapālānāṃ eka eva pṛthagguṇān
translation: Mountains along with their caverns afforded him pleasure-grounds. Trees bore fruits and flowers in all seasons. Singly he combined in himself all the different functions of Lokapālas (such as showering, burning, evaporating etc.).

devanagari: स इत्थं निर्जितककुब् एकराड् वियान् प्रियान् । यथोपजोषं भुञ्जानो नातृप्यद् अजितेन्द्रियः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: sa itthaṃ nirjitakakub ekarāḍ viyān priyānyathopajoṣaṃ bhuñjāno nātṛpyad ajitendriyaḥ
translation: He who became the sole, absolute ruler of the world after vanquishing all the directions, enjoyed as much as he liked, pleasures dear to him. But having no control over senses, he was not satiated thereby.

devanagari: एवं ऐश्वर्यमत्तस्य दृप्तस्योच्छास्त्रवर्तिनः । कालो महान् व्यतीयाय ब्रह्मशापं उपेयुषः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ aiśvaryamattasya dṛptasyocchāstravartinaḥkālo mahān vyatīyāya brahmaśāpaṃ upeyuṣaḥ
translation: In this way, a long period (of more than seventy- one Yugas) passed. With the imprecation of a Brāhmaṇa (Sanaka) hanging over his head, he became inebriated with power and pelf. In his arrogance, he violated the injunctions of the śāstras.

devanagari: तस्योग्रदण्डसंविग्नाः सर्वे लोकाः सपालकाः । अन्यत्रालब्धशरणाः शरणं ययुरच्युतम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyogradaṇḍasaṃvignāḥ sarve lokāḥ sapālakāḥanyatrālabdhaśaraṇāḥ śaraṇaṃ yayuracyutam
translation: Being extremely afraid of his harsh punishments and stern rule, all the inhabitants of the world, along with their protective deities, sought shelter with the unfailing Lord Viṣṇu, as they got no protection anywhere else.

devanagari: तस्यै नमोऽस्तु काष्ठायै यत्रात्मा हरिरीश्वरः । यद्‍गत्वा न निवर्तन्ते शान्ताः संन्यासिनोऽमलाः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyai namo'stu kāṣṭhāyai yatrātmā harirīśvaraḥyad‍gatvā na nivartante śāntāḥ saṃnyāsino'malāḥ
translation: (They prayed) “Salutation to that direction where resides Hari, the Soul and sovereign ruler of the universe—the direction (region) after reaching which, pure and tranquil recluses do not return (to the mundane existence).”

devanagari: इति ते संयतात्मानः समाहितधियोऽमलाः । उपतस्थुर्हृषीकेशं विनिद्रा वायुभोजनाः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: iti te saṃyatātmānaḥ samāhitadhiyo'malāḥupatasthurhṛṣīkeśaṃ vinidrā vāyubhojanāḥ
translation:
[1] Having thus controlled their minds and meditating over the Lord with concentrated attention, they shed off their sins. Foregoing their sleep and subsisting on air only, they prayed Lord Viṣṇu.
[(Additional verse in Padaratnāvalī’s text).]
23(A). Hail to the Supreme Person endowed with six excellences, who is the Inner Controller of all, Omniscient and extremely merciful, and who is absolute knowledge and bliss incarnate, a resort affording absolute protection.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Padaratnāvalī: In this way, feeling confident in mind that the Lord will protect them and with concentrated attention (thus controlling their external and internal senses) and subsisting on air (and controlling the vital breath—prāṇa) and foregoing sleep (thus controlling their bodies), they repeated the following mantra.

devanagari: तेषां आविरभूद्वाणी अरूपा मेघनिःस्वना । सन्नादयन्ती ककुभः साधूनां अभयङ्करी ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: teṣāṃ āvirabhūdvāṇī arūpā meghaniḥsvanāsannādayantī kakubhaḥ sādhūnāṃ abhayaṅkarī
translation: A voice emanating from no visible form, deep like the rumbling of clouds and completely filling all the quarters of heaven with its reverberations, was heard by them, assuring protection to the righteous.

devanagari: मा भैष्ट विबुधश्रेष्ठाः सर्वेषां भद्रमस्तु वः । मद्दर्शनं हि भूतानां सर्वश्रेयोपपत्तये ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: mā bhaiṣṭa vibudhaśreṣṭhāḥ sarveṣāṃ bhadramastu vaḥmaddarśanaṃ hi bhūtānāṃ sarvaśreyopapattaye
translation: They heard: “Oh foremost among the gods! Be not afraid. Good betide you all. A sight of myself (as well as the hearing of my voice) is the means to attain all blessings to all creatures.

devanagari: ज्ञातमेतस्य दौरात्म्यं दैतेयापसदस्य यत् । तस्य शान्तिं करिष्यामि कालं तावत्प्रतीक्षत ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: jñātametasya daurātmyaṃ daiteyāpasadasya yattasya śāntiṃ kariṣyāmi kālaṃ tāvatpratīkṣata
translation: The wickedness and oppression of this wretched Daitya is already known (to me). I shall mete out a suitable pacification (death) to him. Wait till that period.

devanagari: यदा देवेषु वेदेषु गोषु विप्रेषु साधुषु । धर्मे मयि च विद्वेषः स वा आशु विनश्यति ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: yadā deveṣu vedeṣu goṣu vipreṣu sādhuṣudharme mayi ca vidveṣaḥ sa vā āśu vinaśyati
translation: When a person cherishes hatred to gods, the Vedas, cows, Brāhmaṇas, the righteous people, religion and Me, he verily perishes in no time.

devanagari: निर्वैराय प्रशान्ताय स्वसुताय महात्मने । प्रह्रादाय यदा द्रुह्येद् हनिष्येऽपि वरोर्जितम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: nirvairāya praśāntāya svasutāya mahātmaneprahrādāya yadā druhyed haniṣye'pi varorjitam
translation: When he (Hiraṇyakaśipu) will maliciously seek to injure his own son, the serene high-souled Prahlāda who cherishes no enmity against anyone, I shall slay him despite his power derived from Brahmā’s boons.”

devanagari: नारद उवाचइत्युक्ता लोकगुरुणा तं प्रणम्य दिवौकसः । न्यवर्तन्त गतोद्वेगा मेनिरे चासुरं हतम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: nārada uvācaityuktā lokaguruṇā taṃ praṇamya divaukasaḥnyavartanta gatodvegā menire cāsuraṃ hatam
translation:
Nārada said:
Thus assured (addressed) by the Father (and the protector) of the world, the inhabitants of heaven (gods) bowed down to hin. With all their anxieties allayed, they returned, deeming that the demon is (as good as) killed.

devanagari: तस्य दैत्यपतेः पुत्राश्चत्वारः परमाद्‍भुताः । प्रह्रादोऽभून् महांन् तेषां गुणैर्महदुपासकः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: tasya daityapateḥ putrāścatvāraḥ paramād‍bhutāḥprahrādo'bhūn mahāṃn teṣāṃ guṇairmahadupāsakaḥ
translation: The king of Daityas had four extremely marvellous sons. Prahlāda who was the worshipper of the Exalted Being was preeminent amongst them by his virtues.

devanagari: ब्रह्मण्यः शीलसम्पन्नः सत्यसन्धो जितेन्द्रियः । आत्मवत्सर्वभूतानां एकः प्रियसुहृत्तमः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmaṇyaḥ śīlasampannaḥ satyasandho jitendriyaḥātmavatsarvabhūtānāṃ ekaḥ priyasuhṛttamaḥ
translation: He was full of reverence to Brāhmaṇas and was possessed of noble character, true to his word, self-controlled, and like Paramātman, he was the best friend and well-wisher of all.

devanagari: दासवत्सन्नतार्याङ्घ्रिः पितृवद् दीनवत्सलः । भ्रातृवत्सदृशे स्निग्धो गुरुषु ईश्वरभावनः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: dāsavatsannatāryāṅghriḥ pitṛvad dīnavatsalaḥbhrātṛvatsadṛśe snigdho guruṣu īśvarabhāvanaḥ
translation: Like a servant, he bowed at the feet of noble Souls. Like a father he was kind to the poor and needy. With equals, he was affectionate like a brother. He looked upon his elders as his masters. Though he was highly endowed with learning, munificence, personal charms and high lineage, he was completely free from pride and arrogance.

devanagari: नोद्विग्नचित्तो व्यसनेषु निःस्पृहः । श्रुतेषु दृष्टेषु गुणेष्ववस्तुदृक् । दान्तेन्द्रियप्राणशरीरधीः सदा । प्रशान्तकामो रहितासुरोऽसुरः ॥ ...
english_devnagari: nodvignacitto vyasaneṣu niḥspṛhaḥśruteṣu dṛṣṭeṣu guṇeṣvavastudṛkdāntendriyaprāṇaśarīradhīḥ sadāpraśāntakāmo rahitāsuro'suraḥ
translation: In dangers and calamities, his mind (always) remained unruffled. He cherished no desire for enjoyments, heard (as obtainable in the next world) or seen (in this world), as he regarded them to be unreal. He had controlled his senses, vital airs (the life-breath), body and intellect. He completely controlled his desires and kept his mind calm and serene. Though he was a demon by birth, he was devoid of demonic characteristics.

devanagari: यस्मिन्महद्‍गुणा राजन् गृह्यन्ते कविभिर्मुहुः । न तेऽधुना पिधीयन्ते यथा भगवतीश्वरे ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: yasminmahad‍guṇā rājan gṛhyante kavibhirmuhuḥna te'dhunā pidhīyante yathā bhagavatīśvare
translation: Oh King! The excellent qualities of the Lord or of his devotees are now and again extolled by the wise. Even now they are not obscured as is the case with the excellences of the Lord.

devanagari: यं साधुगाथासदसि रिपवोऽपि सुरा नृप । प्रतिमानं प्रकुर्वन्ति किमुतान्ये भवादृशाः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: yaṃ sādhugāthāsadasi ripavo'pi surā nṛpapratimānaṃ prakurvanti kimutānye bhavādṛśāḥ
translation: In assemblies, where the discourses on the appreciation of the righteous take place, gods, Prahlāda’s sworn enemies, cite him as a model (of piety and righteousness). What to speak about the devotees of Viṣṇu like you?

devanagari: गुणैरलमसंख्येयै माहात्म्यं तस्य सूच्यते । वासुदेवे भगवति यस्य नैसर्गिकी रतिः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: guṇairalamasaṃkhyeyai māhātmyaṃ tasya sūcyatevāsudeve bhagavati yasya naisargikī ratiḥ
translation: Prahlāda had a natural love and devotion to the glorious Lord Vāsudeva. Hence it is impossible to describe his innumerable qualities. The description of his nobleness of Soul (and greatness) is only suggestive (and not exhaustive).

devanagari: न्यस्तक्रीडनको बालो जडवत् तन्मनस्तया । कृष्णग्रहगृहीतात्मा न वेद जगदीदृशम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: nyastakrīḍanako bālo jaḍavat tanmanastayākṛṣṇagrahagṛhītātmā na veda jagadīdṛśam
translation: Even as a child, he set aside his toys. His mind being completely absorbed in the Lord, he appeared as a dunce. As his Soul (mind) was possessed by Kṛṣṇa, as though like a spirit, its coveted treasure, he did not know the matter-of-fact world (as known to ordinary persons, but was full of his presence). (His Soul was completely absorbed in the contemplation of Kṛṣṇa who was his only object of attraction).

devanagari: आसीनः पर्यटन् अश्नन् शयानः प्रपिबन् ब्रुवन् । नानुसन्धत्त एतानि गोविन्दपरिरम्भितः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: āsīnaḥ paryaṭan aśnan śayānaḥ prapiban bruvannānusandhatta etāni govindaparirambhitaḥ
translation: Being folded in the embrace of Viṣṇu (i.e. finding himself fully identified with the Lord), he was not conscious of his acts such as sitting, walking, eating, sleeping, drinking or speaking.

devanagari: क्वचिद् रुदति वैकुण्ठ चिन्ताशबलचेतनः । क्वचिद् हसति तच्चिन्ता ह्लाद उद्‍गायति क्वचित् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: kvacid rudati vaikuṇṭha cintāśabalacetanaḥkvacid hasati taccintā hlāda ud‍gāyati kvacit
translation: Sometimes his consciousness would get mixed with anxiety for the Lord, and being agitated, he cried. Sometimes he would laugh in the excitement of his meditation about him; sometimes he would sing loudly.

devanagari: नदति क्वचिद् उत्कण्ठो विलज्जो नृत्यति क्वचित् । क्वचित्तद्‍भावनायुक्तः तन्मयोऽनुचकार ह ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: nadati kvacid utkaṇṭho vilajjo nṛtyati kvacitkvacittad‍bhāvanāyuktaḥ tanmayo'nucakāra ha
translation: Sometimes he shouted at the top of his voice. Sometimes, banishing all the sense of bashfulness, he would dance about. Sometimes, being filled with thoughts about him and feeling absorbed in him completely, he would imitate him.

devanagari: क्वचिदुत्पुलकस्तूष्णीं आस्ते संस्पर्शनिर्वृतः । अस्पन्दप्रणयानन्द सलिलामीलितेक्षणः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: kvacidutpulakastūṣṇīṃ āste saṃsparśanirvṛtaḥaspandapraṇayānanda salilāmīlitekṣaṇaḥ
translation: Sometimes, with his hair standing on end, he would sit silent as if (deeply) satisfied with the Divine touch, (as if the Lord manifested himself in his heart and touched him with his hand). Sometimes, his eyes were half-closed and steady, with tears of joy and love flowing out.

devanagari: स उत्तमश्लोकपदारविन्दयोः । निषेवयाकिञ्चनसङ्गलब्धया । तन्वन् परां निर्वृतिमात्मनो मुहुः । दुसङ्गदीनस्य मनः शमं व्यधात् ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: sa uttamaślokapadāravindayoḥniṣevayākiñcanasaṅgalabdhayātanvan parāṃ nirvṛtimātmano muhuḥdusaṅgadīnasya manaḥ śamaṃ vyadhāt
translation: By the service of the lotus-like feet of Lord of hallowing glory (Viṣṇu) which he acquired while in association with the Lord’s devotees who claim nothing as their own, he attained supreme happiness not only to himself but often imparted that felicity to the minds of others which were polluted and miserable by bad company.

devanagari: तस्मिन् महाभागवते महाभागे महात्मनि । हिरण्यकशिपू राजन् अकरोद् अघमात्मजे ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmin mahābhāgavate mahābhāge mahātmanihiraṇyakaśipū rājan akarod aghamātmaje
translation: To his son, who was such a great devotee of the Lord, magnanimous and a highly noble Soul, that Hiraṇyakaśipu tried to do harm. Oh king.

devanagari: युधिष्ठिर उवाचदेवर्ष एतदिच्छामो वेदितुं तव सुव्रत । यदात्मजाय शुद्धाय पितादात्साधवे ह्यघम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: yudhiṣṭhira uvācadevarṣa etadicchāmo vedituṃ tava suvratayadātmajāya śuddhāya pitādātsādhave hyagham
translation:
Yudhiṣṭhira said:
Oh divine sage of excellent vows! We desire to know from you why the father meted out ill-treatment to his son who was so pure, innocent and righteous.

devanagari: पुत्रान् विप्रतिकूलान् स्वान् पितरः पुत्रवत्सलाः । उपालभन्ते शिक्षार्थं नैवाघमपरो यथा ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: putrān vipratikūlān svān pitaraḥ putravatsalāḥupālabhante śikṣārthaṃ naivāghamaparo yathā
translation: Fathers affectionate to their sons do remonstrate their disobedient sons for the sake of imparting education but never harm any like an enemy.

devanagari: किमुतानुवशान् साधून् तादृशान् गुरुदेवतान् । एतत्कौतूहलं ब्रह्मन् अस्माकं विधम प्रभो । पितुः पुत्राय यद्द्वेषो मरणाय प्रयोजितः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: kimutānuvaśān sādhūn tādṛśān gurudevatānetatkautūhalaṃ brahman asmākaṃ vidhama prabhopituḥ putrāya yaddveṣo maraṇāya prayojitaḥ
translation:
How much less would they do so in the case of such sons who are so obedient, righteous and regard the father as a deity.
Oh Brāhmaṇa sage, be pleased to satisfy our curiosity as to how the hatred of the father towards his son culminated in the father’s attempt on the life of his son (or became the cause of the father’s death), My Lord.
[back to top]

devanagari: देवोद्यानश्रिया जुष्टं अध्यास्ते स्म त्रिपिष्टपम् । महेन्द्रभवनं साक्षात् निर्मितं विश्वकर्मणा । त्रैलोक्यलक्ष्म्यायतनं अध्युवासाखिलर्द्...
english_devnagari: devodyānaśriyā juṣṭaṃ adhyāste sma tripiṣṭapammahendrabhavanaṃ sākṣāt nirmitaṃ viśvakarmaṇātrailokyalakṣmyāyatanaṃ adhyuvāsākhilarddhimat

devanagari: विद्यार्थरूपजन्माढ्यो मानस्तम्भविवर्जितः ॥ ३२३ ॥
english_devnagari: vidyārtharūpajanmāḍhyo mānastambhavivarjitaḥ
